body.title: Wire trust-hierarchy multiplier into Librarian confidence gate
body.result: trust multiplier wired into _effective_sources via _load_principals from palace config
body.description:
Trusted humans function as credibility multipliers in the Librarian's confidence gate: Akien's assertion that X is true should count as many independent sources, allowing high-confidence writes without requiring multiple external corroborations.

The trusted_principals config is written by T-librarian-constitutional-layer. This ticket wires it into the confidence gate from T-librarian-palace-confidence-gate: when a write's provenance_chain includes an attribution to a trusted principal, multiply the effective source count by that principal's credibility_multiplier before evaluating the tier threshold.

Example: Akien (multiplier=10) asserting a claim → effective source count = 10 → clears MEDIUM threshold (requires 2) and HIGH threshold (requires 5) without additional external sources.

**Affected files:** agent_datacenter/devices/librarian/palace_writer.py (or wherever T-librarian-palace-confidence-gate lands) — MEDIUM inertia; palace config read (LOW inertia)
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/coding (MEDIUM inertia); theigors/rules/database (read from clan.memory_palace via psycopg2 or MCP)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: read trusted_principals palace node + apply multiplier in confidence gate evaluation. Out of scope: changing the trusted_principals content (palace edit); adding new principals (Akien's job via palace write).
**Test plan:** With Akien (multiplier=10) in provenance_chain of a write, the write clears MEDIUM tier with 1 attributed source. A claim with unknown principal gets no multiplier. memory_get(path='librarian/config/trusted_principals') is the config source.
